summary: First end-to-end test for GLASSWORK MVP. Backend: 31/31 pytest tests passed covering auth, projects, materials, material-entries, daily-logs, photos, dashboard, alerts, users, reports, and role separation. Frontend: verified admin & worker flows via mobile viewport (390x844) - login, dashboard KPIs, projects list, project detail, alerts, team, reports, worker home, worker daily-log form, worker material register. Role separation fully enforced: worker sees no €/budget anywhere and has correct tab bar (Inicio/Obras/Parte/Historial/Perfil).

issues: Console warnings: 'shadow*' style props deprecated (use boxShadow); props.pointerEvents deprecated (use style.pointerEvents). Non-blocking but noisy in logs.

react_native_issues.issue: useNativeDriver not supported on web → falls back to JS animation. Expected for web preview, no action needed.

action_items: (Optional polish) Suppress deprecation warnings by replacing shadow* with boxShadow and pointerEvents prop with style.pointerEvents.

(Optional) Backend test run created leaked seed data: 2 TEST_New Worker users, extra weekly reports, and test material/photo/log entries. Consider a cleanup fixture or prefix filter before production demo.

(Optional) Expo package version mismatches flagged by Metro (expo-camera, expo-haptics, expo-image-manipulator, expo-image-picker, expo-local-authentication, expo-secure-store). Not blocking web preview but may affect native builds.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1237 lines — consider splitting into routers (auth, projects, materials, logs, reports) for maintainability.
Seed logic runs on startup if DB empty — idempotent enough, but tests rely on exactly 4 projects / 3 alerts / 2 reports. Leaked test data bumps these counts; tests wisely use >= assertions.
MongoDB _id is correctly excluded across responses (spot-checked list endpoints).
JWT secret hardcoded default is avoided (.env has a real secret). Good.
Role guards confirmed at API layer (worker cannot hit /dashboard/summary, POST /users, or review logs).
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created a few TEST_-prefixed worker users, one daily log, one material entry, one photo, and one generated weekly report via POST endpoints. These persist in Mongo; harmless but visible in UI lists.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend route for daily-logs expects 'hours_worked' and 'work_description' (NOT 'hours'/'description'). Project card testIDs are 'mgr-project-{id}' (manager) and 'worker-project-{id}' (worker), not 'project-card-{id}'. Worker /projects tab bar route redirects/renders home screen content via (worker)/projects.tsx — worked fine. Frontend BASE URL: EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=https://site-glass-preview.preview.emergentagent.com. Seed data may now include extra TEST_ users/reports from backend tests. All 31 pytest tests pass; run via: python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v.
rca_of_issue: No blocker issues. Initial 3 test failures were due to my test payload using 'hours'/'description' while backend schema uses 'hours_worked'/'work_description'. Fixed payload → 31/31 pass. This is a test-side correction, not a product bug; frontend already sends the correct field names (verified in /app/frontend/app/log/new.tsx).
